title: BetterChatHeads
description: BetterChatHeads adds player heads next to chat messages, making multiplayer chat clearer, more visual, and easier to read.
body:
Better Chat Heads

A high-performance, client-side chat visual enhancement utility for the Fabric ecosystem.

--- ### **Overview** **Better Chat Heads** seamlessly injects real-time player head avatars next to chat messages within multiplayer environments. Engineered to enhance spatial awareness and visual readability in high-traffic chat streams, this client-side utility features intelligent skin synchronization pipelines and advanced regex pattern matching to support complex, rank-heavy server message formats without impacting frame rendering times. --- ### **Key Features** * **Contextual Avatar Injection** — Dynamically renders player heads alongside validated multiplayer chat vectors, fully supporting secondary skin layers, overlays, hats, and hair geometry. * **Smart Skin Sync+ & Live Swap** — Features an automated retry pipeline for initially unresolved skin textures, transitioning smoothly from default asset fallbacks (Steve/Alex) to verified player skins in real-time. * **Per-Server Profile Isolation** — Automatically preserves unique configuration parameters customized to individual server environments. * **Adaptive Pattern Ingestion** — Utilizes a local learning cache to dynamically index, memorize, and parse server-specific chat prefixes, rank structures, and custom message separators. * **Granular UI Scaling** — Includes options for compact layout modes, custom head introduction animations, and adjustable frame-timing variables. * **Zero Server Overhead** — Operates entirely within the local client runtime. Features an integrated configuration panel and optional Mod Menu compatibility with instant state saving. --- ### **Installation Guide** 1. **Dependency Verification:** Ensure the target environment has the correct iteration of the **Fabric API** installed. 2. **Deployment:** Place the compiled `BetterChatHeads` binary payload directly into your local `mods` directory. 3. **Initialization:** Launch the runtime client and access the localized configuration panel layout to adjust pacing and cache rules. ---

Available exclusively for the Fabric Mod Loader ecosystem.
